title: oryzopsis gracilis
intro: Oryzopsis gracilis, also known as blue wildrye, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Oryzopsis gracilis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as an herbal infusion, tincture, or salve, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine.
